enonce.texte: Match the vocabulary words with their correct definitions.

questions.texte: Match 'crime' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: An action that is against the law.

questions.texte: Match 'violence' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: Physical force intended to hurt or damage someone or something.

questions.texte: Match 'misbehavior' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: Bad or unacceptable behavior, especially by a child or young person.

questions.texte: Match 'suspension' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: The temporary removal of a student from school as a punishment.

questions.texte: Match 'punishment' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: A penalty imposed on someone for breaking a rule or law.

questions.texte: Match 'rehabilitation' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: The process of helping someone to return to a normal, healthy life after a period of bad behavior or imprisonment.

questions.texte: Match 'delinquency' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: Minor crime, especially committed by young people.

questions.texte: Match 'criminal' with its correct definition.
questions.reponse: A person who has committed a crime.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: Juvenile Delinquency in Cameroon

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

Text: Juvenile Delinquency in Cameroon

Juvenile delinquency is a growing concern in Cameroon. Many young people are getting involved in criminal activities such as theft, drug abuse, and violence. Several factors contribute to this problem. Poverty is one of the main causes. Many children come from poor families and cannot afford to go to school. They end up on the streets where they are exposed to bad influences. Lack of parental supervision is another factor. Parents who work long hours often do not have time to monitor their children's activities. Peer pressure also plays an important role. Young people often imitate their friends who engage in criminal behavior. The consequences of juvenile delinquency are serious. Young offenders may face suspension or expulsion from school. They may also be sent to juvenile detention centers. Many of them struggle to reintegrate into society after their punishment. Experts believe that prevention is better than punishment. They recommend providing more educational and employment opportunities for young people. They also suggest that parents should spend more time with their children and that communities should organize activities to keep young people engaged.
images: A group of young people participating in a community activity.

questions.texte: Identify the main causes of juvenile delinquency mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: The main causes are: poverty, lack of parental supervision, and peer pressure.

questions.texte: Explain why poverty can lead to juvenile delinquency.
questions.reponse: Poverty leads to juvenile delinquency because many children from poor families cannot afford to go to school. They end up on the streets where they are exposed to bad influences and criminal activities.

questions.texte: Analyze the consequences of juvenile delinquency for young offenders.
questions.reponse: The consequences include suspension or expulsion from school, being sent to juvenile detention centers, and difficulty reintegrating into society after punishment.

questions.texte: What solutions does the text propose to address the problem of juvenile delinquency?
questions.reponse: The text proposes providing more educational and employment opportunities, parents spending more time with their children, and communities organizing activities to keep young people engaged.

questions.texte: Evaluate the statement: 'Prevention is better than punishment.' Do you agree? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent argumenter que la prévention est plus efficace car elle s'attaque aux causes profondes du problème et aide les jeunes à éviter la criminalité, tandis que la punition intervient après les faits.

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct vocabulary words from the box.

Words: crime, violence, misbehavior, suspension, punishment, rehabilitation, delinquency, criminal

questions.texte: The young boy was arrested for ________ after he stole a bicycle.
questions.reponse: crime

questions.texte: ________ in schools is a serious problem that needs to be addressed.
questions.reponse: Violence

questions.texte: The teacher warned the student about his constant ________ in class.
questions.reponse: misbehavior

questions.texte: He received a one-week ________ for fighting with another student.
questions.reponse: suspension

questions.texte: The court decided that ________ was more appropriate than imprisonment for the young offender.
questions.reponse: rehabilitation

questions.texte: The ________ was sentenced to five years in prison for his crimes.
questions.reponse: criminal

questions.texte: ________ is a serious issue that affects many communities in Cameroon.
questions.reponse: Delinquency

questions.texte: The judge gave the young offender a light ________ because it was his first offense.
questions.reponse: punishment

intitule: Grammar: Modal Verbs for Prevention and Punishment

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct modal verbs: must, should, can, could, might.

questions.texte: Parents ________ monitor their children's activities to prevent delinquency.
questions.reponse: should

questions.texte: Schools ________ provide counseling for students who show signs of misbehavior.
questions.reponse: should

questions.texte: Young people ________ avoid bad company to stay out of trouble.
questions.reponse: should

questions.texte: The government ________ create more educational opportunities for youth.
questions.reponse: must

questions.texte: Community programs ________ help keep young people engaged and away from crime.
questions.reponse: can

questions.texte: Parents ________ find it difficult to control their teenagers.
questions.reponse: might

questions.texte: Young offenders ________ be given a second chance to change their behavior.
questions.reponse: should

questions.texte: We ________ all work together to prevent juvenile delinquency.
questions.reponse: must

enonce.texte: You are a youth worker in your community. You want to create a program to prevent juvenile delinquency. Read the tasks below and complete them.
images: A poster about a youth program with activities for young people.

questions.texte: Identify three risk factors for juvenile delinquency in your community. Explain why each factor is a risk.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent identifier des facteurs comme la pauvreté, le manque de supervision parentale, la pression des pairs, ou l'absence d'activités constructives.

questions.texte: Design a prevention program for young people in your community. What activities would you include?
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent proposer des activités comme des sports, des ateliers de formation, des programmes de mentorat, des activités culturelles, etc.

questions.texte: Explain why your program would be effective in preventing juvenile delinquency.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent expliquer que le programme offre des alternatives positives, occupe les jeunes, et leur donne des compétences pour la vie.

questions.texte: Evaluate the role of the community in preventing juvenile delinquency. Why is community involvement important?
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent expliquer que la communauté joue un rôle crucial en offrant du soutien, en créant des opportunités et en veillant au bien-être des jeunes.

intitule: Case Study: A Young Offender's Story

enonce.texte:
Read the case study and answer the questions.

Case Study: Jean-Pierre's Story

Jean-Pierre is a 15-year-old boy living in a poor neighborhood in Douala. His father left the family when he was young, and his mother works long hours to support the family. Jean-Pierre started hanging out with a group of older boys who encouraged him to skip school. Soon, he was involved in petty theft and drug use. He was arrested twice for stealing. The school suspended him, and he spent three months in a juvenile detention center. After his release, he wanted to change his life. He joined a community program that offered vocational training. He learned how to repair electronics. Now, he has a job and is staying away from crime.
images: A young man repairing electronics in a workshop.

questions.texte: Identify the risk factors that contributed to Jean-Pierre's delinquency.
questions.reponse: The risk factors are poverty, lack of parental supervision, and peer pressure from older boys.

questions.texte: Analyze the consequences Jean-Pierre faced because of his delinquency.
questions.reponse: He was arrested twice, suspended from school, and spent three months in a juvenile detention center.

questions.texte: What helped Jean-Pierre change his life after his release from detention?
questions.reponse: He joined a community program that offered vocational training in electronics repair. This gave him a skill and a job.

questions.texte: Propose three actions that could have prevented Jean-Pierre from becoming involved in crime.
questions.reponse: Individuelle réponse. Exemples: 1. Programmes de mentorat pour les jeunes à risque. 2. Activités parascolaires pour occuper les jeunes. 3. Soutien aux familles en difficulté.

enonce.texte:
Read the statement and answer the questions.

Statement: "Juvenile offenders should be punished harshly to deter them from committing crimes again. Rehabilitation is a waste of resources."

questions.texte: Analyze the arguments for harsh punishment for juvenile offenders.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent mentionner que la punition sévère peut dissuader les jeunes de commettre des crimes et protéger la société.

questions.texte: Analyze the arguments for rehabilitation instead of punishment.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent mentionner que la réhabilitation donne une seconde chance aux jeunes, les aide à changer de comportement et réduit la récidive.

questions.texte: Evaluate which approach is more effective in your opinion. Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent argumenter en faveur de la réhabilitation parce qu'elle s'attaque aux causes profondes du comportement délinquant et offre des solutions à long terme.

questions.texte: What do you think should be done to prevent juvenile delinquency? Use modal verbs in your answer.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent utiliser 'should', 'must', 'can' pour proposer des solutions. Exemple: 'Parents should spend more time with their children. Schools must provide counseling services. Communities can organize activities for young people.'

enonce.texte: Complete the table by forming the correct word from the base word provided.

questions.texte: crime (noun) → ________ (adjective)
questions.reponse: criminal

questions.texte: violence (noun) → ________ (adjective)
questions.reponse: violent

questions.texte: punish (verb) → ________ (noun)
questions.reponse: punishment

questions.texte: suspend (verb) → ________ (noun)
questions.reponse: suspension

questions.texte: rehabilitate (verb) → ________ (noun)
questions.reponse: rehabilitation

questions.texte: delinquent (adjective) → ________ (noun)
questions.reponse: delinquency

questions.texte: behave (verb) → ________ (noun)
questions.reponse: behavior

questions.texte: criminal (noun) → ________ (adverb)
questions.reponse: criminally

intitule: Reading and Writing: A Letter to a Young Offender

enonce.texte:
Read the text and complete the tasks below.

Text: A Message of Hope

Jean-Pierre, you have made mistakes, but you are not a bad person. Everyone deserves a second chance. Your past does not define you. You have shown that you can change by learning a new skill and finding a job. Your story is an inspiration to other young people who are going through similar struggles. Remember that your future is in your hands. Stay away from bad company and focus on your goals. You are a valuable member of our community, and we are proud of you.
images: A motivational poster with a message of hope for young people.

questions.texte: What message does the text convey to Jean-Pierre?
questions.reponse: The text conveys a message of hope and encouragement. It tells Jean-Pierre that he is not defined by his past and deserves a second chance.

questions.texte: Explain why the text says, 'Your past does not define you.'
questions.reponse: It means that Jean-Pierre should not be judged solely by his mistakes. He has the ability to change and create a better future for himself.

questions.texte: Imagine you are a youth counselor writing a letter to a young person who is at risk of delinquency. Write a short letter (about 100 words) encouraging them to make positive choices.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent écrire une lettre encourageante avec des conseils pratiques et un message d'espoir.

questions.texte: What advice would you give to a young person who is facing peer pressure to engage in criminal activities? Use vocabulary from the lesson.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent utiliser des mots comme 'peer pressure', 'misbehavior', 'consequences' pour donner des conseils sur la façon de résister à la pression des pairs.
